content: A deep active agent’s uncertainty-based memory selection mechanism can be reframed as an implicit form of curiosity regulation: the agent prioritizes storing uncertain memory samples not just to improve downstream task performance, but to self-tune its curiosity drive to avoid both under-exploration (from ignoring high-uncertainty experiences) and distraction (from overloading on noisy, uninformative uncertain outliers). This creates a closed-loop system where memory management directly shapes intrinsic motivation.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
trigger_topic: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
